input.title: Agent host session subscriptions are never released after the chat is closed
input.body:
## Problem

Once the user opens an agent host session in the chat panel, the `IAgentSubscription<ISessionState>` acquired by [`AgentHostSessionHandler`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts) is never released — it stays open for the lifetime of the window (or until the chat content provider unregisters).

Trace:

1. `provideChatSessionContent` calls `_ensureSessionSubscription(resolvedSession)` (around [agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L450](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L450)) and stores the released ref in the `AgentHostChatSession`'s onDispose callback ([line ~526](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L526)).
2. The `IChatSession` is cached by `ChatSessionsService._sessions` in [`getOrCreateChatSession`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatSessions/chatSessions.contribution.ts#L1012).
3. The cache entry is only disposed when (a) the content provider unregisters, (b) a contribution's when-clause turns it off, or (c) the session itself fires `onWillDispose`. **There is no editor-close hook.**

So closing the chat editor doesn't dispose the `IChatSession`, which means `_releaseSessionSubscription` is never called, which means the connection-layer refcount never reaches zero, which means we keep receiving full `ISessionState` delta envelopes (turns, tool calls, queued messages, etc.) for that session for the rest of the window's life.

## Scope

This is **not** a "subscribe to all running sessions on startup" problem — the session list view is fed by `listSessions()` summaries plus connection-level `SessionAdded` / `SessionRemoved` notifications, and never opens per-session subscriptions on its own. Only the chat content path (and now the running-session config picker provider) takes per-session subscriptions.

The cost grows linearly with the number of sessions the user has clicked into during a window's lifetime.

## Expected behavior

Drop the subscription when the chat editor closes, **but** keep it alive while the session is still running so background updates (tool-complete, turn-complete, status changes, auto-rename, etc.) continue to flow for sessions the user might come back to.

Concretely:

- **Chat editor closes** → if the session is in a terminal state, release immediately. Otherwise, arm a one-shot listener that releases on the next terminal-state transition.
- **Session reaches terminal state** → if the chat editor is already closed, release.

## Open question

What counts as "terminal"? `Done` / archived seems right. `Idle` is too aggressive — sessions sit `Idle` between turns and the user might be coming back to send another message in 30 seconds.

## Notes

- The connection-layer refcounting in `AgentSubscriptionManager` already supports this cleanly — this is purely a question of when the chat handler releases its ref.
- The `ChatSessionsService._sessions` cache lifecycle is a separate (related) question: today an `IChatSession` is created on first open and never invalidated when the editor closes. The fix here may need to either hook into editor-close at the `ChatSessionsService` level, or leave the `IChatSession` cached but have the handler scope the *subscription* lifetime more tightly than the `IChatSession` itself.

(Written by Copilot)

input.title: Respect the user's preferred shell in agent host terminals
input.body: Local, remotes are more complicated than I thought, created separate follow up issues, references below in history. Today, host-managed terminals in the agent host (the PTY-backed `bash`/`powershell` tools in [`copilotShellTools.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotShellTools.ts)) hardcode the shell — on Windows that's always windows pwsh (pwsh 5) regardless of what the user has set up. > If I have PowerShell 7 installed I think agent host terminal would still go to windows powershell (pwsh5) Making the default better (well... some windows users are passionate git bash users all time) and also respecting user's preferred shell (such as the one defined in setting) would be great. We allow user to set per OS like: ` "terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows":` - **(a)** Inherit the existing integrated-terminal settings — `terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.*` - The agent host can't read workbench config directly?? because it's a separate Node process, but the workbench-side `NodeAgentHostStarter` already has `IConfigurationService` and forwards data to the child via env vars and the AHP root-config bag — same channel `customizations` uses today. So the workbench resolves the profile via `ITerminalProfileResolverService` and pushes the resolved path/args down on startup and on setting change. - **(b)** Add a dedicated `agentHost.defaultShell` setting. Simple and isolated, but creates a parallel surface users have to configure separately. - **(c)** Both — dedicated setting wins if explicitly set, otherwise inherit from `terminal.integrated.*`. Maybe we'd have dedicated setting for agent host terminal, but if the value is not set there we'll respect user's default terminal profile setting. Existing behavior on Copilot CLI on chat panel:

input.title: AgentHost: Selection attachments drop selected text and range
input.body:
(Codex) 

## Problem

The Copilot CLI chat-panel path preserves editor selection context when sending a request: it forwards the selected file, selected text, and selection range.

The Agent Host path currently converts an implicit selection into a selection attachment, but only includes the file URI and display name. The selected text and range are dropped before the request reaches the Copilot SDK.

## Current Context

There are incoming agent host protocol changes for richer attachments. This issue should track the VS Code-side implementation work after those changes land, rather than extending the current older `file` / `directory` / `selection` attachment shape directly.

## Evidence

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts`
  - `_convertVariablesToAttachments` handles implicit selections, but emits only `{ type, uri, displayName }` today.
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/common/agentService.ts`
  - `IAgentAttachment` already has optional `text` and `selection` fields in the current VS Code-side interface.
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotAgentSession.ts`
  - `CopilotAgentSession.send` already forwards `text` and `selection` to the public Copilot SDK if present.
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/chatSessions/copilotcli/node/copilotcliPromptResolver.ts`
  - The existing chat-panel CLI path preserves richer selection context.

## Expected Behavior

When a user sends a Copilot CLI request through Agent Host with editor selection context, the Agent Host path should preserve:

- selected file URI/path
- selected text
- selection start/end line and character

This should match the current Copilot CLI chat-panel behavior and avoid losing important context after moving CLI integration to Agent Host.

## Likely Fix

After the richer attachment shape lands, wire editor selection attachments through the VS Code Agent Host path:

- Convert implicit editor selection variables into the richer selection attachment shape.
- Preserve selected text and selection range when converting chat variables in `AgentHostSessionHandler`.
- Preserve those fields through Agent Host side effects/state.
- Map the richer Agent Host selection attachment into the Copilot SDK selection attachment shape in `CopilotAgentSession.send`.

## Classification

Agent Host implementation gap. The public Copilot SDK already accepts selection `text` and `selection` fields; the remaining work is VS Code-side Agent Host wiring after the incoming agent host protocol changes land.

input.title: AgentHost: Terminal opens vim then hangs
Allow Claude Agent to run through Anthropic subscription instead of Copilot quota
input.body: I would like VS Code to support using **Claude Agent (Preview)** directly through an **Anthropic / Claude subscription or Anthropic API credentials**, instead of only consuming the user’s **GitHub Copilot subscription**. This feature request comes from a real misunderstanding I had while using the feature. Since Claude Agent is described as using the official Anthropic agent harness / Claude Agent SDK, I initially assumed that the Claude usage would be tied to my existing Claude/Anthropic subscription, or at least that there would be a way to use it directly through Anthropic. The confusion mainly came from the agent/model selector, where seeing Claude Agent listed made me assume that it could be used through my Claude/Anthropic subscription. Because I misunderstood this billing model, I ended up spending around **$300 extra in requests in a single week**. This is not meant as a complaint, but as context for why making the billing/authentication model more explicit, or supporting direct Anthropic usage, would be valuable. Ideally, Claude Agent could offer a provider/authentication option such as: - Use via GitHub Copilot subscription - Use via Anthropic / Claude subscription - Use via Anthropic API key or Claude Code authentication This would allow users to choose explicitly how Claude Agent usage is authenticated and billed. If supporting direct Anthropic authentication inside the existing Claude Agent option is not desirable, I would also see it as a viable alternative to add a separate option to the current agent/model selector, clearly indicating that this version runs through the user’s Anthropic/Claude subscription instead of Copilot. For example, the selector could include two clearly separated entries: - Claude Agent — via Copilot subscription - Claude Agent — via Anthropic subscription This would make the billing model explicit and avoid confusion, while still preserving the current Copilot-based integration as the default or recommended option. From a user perspective, this seems potentially feasible, since the existing Claude extension for VS Code already allows users to work with Claude through their own Claude/Anthropic access. I understand there may be product, technical, or legal considerations on the VS Code/Copilot side, but having a similar option in the integrated chat/agent selector would be much more convenient. The main benefit would be that developers who already use Claude or Claude Code could use the full Claude Agent SDK experience inside VS Code without consuming Copilot quota or premium requests. It would also make the integration more flexible and clearer, especially for users who want to keep Copilot and Anthropic usage separate. Even if direct Anthropic authentication is not planned, it would be very helpful to make the billing/usage model more explicit in the UI, so users do not mistakenly assume that Claude Agent usage is covered by their Claude subscription.

input.title: Agent host CLI: kill_running_server leaks server node child process after upgrade
input.body:
## Summary

The CLI agent host supervisor's `kill_running_server` path leaks the underlying server node process when restarting after a `_vscodeUpgrade` RPC. The bash launcher script (`<server>/bin/code-server-<quality>`) is terminated, but the `node ... server-main.js` child it `spawn`'d remains alive as an orphan.

## Repro

Discovered while testing https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/319455 against a remote macOS host using a pinned-old-server CLI:

1. Bring up an agent host supervisor on commit A (e.g. `7090241a056cb49cfe90f58da9a8cc44df9262a9`, protocol 0.2.0).
2. Connect a desktop on commit B (newer protocol 0.3.0). Handshake fails with `UnsupportedProtocolVersion`.
3. Click "Update Server". The supervisor receives the `_vscodeUpgrade` RPC, calls `kill_running_server`, then restarts on commit B.
4. Observe processes on the remote: the bash wrapper for commit A is gone, but its node child (e.g. `~/.<serverDataFolderName>/cli/servers/Insiders-7090241a.../server/node ... server-main.js`) is still alive with `ppid=1`.

In my test run the orphan was pid 230 and survived indefinitely; only the new commit-B server's child was tracked by the supervisor after the restart.

## Suspected cause

`cli/src/tunnels/agent_host.rs::kill_running_server` (or the platform-specific kill helper it delegates to) appears to signal the immediate child only. The server launcher is:

```
<server>/bin/code-server-<quality>      (bash)
  └── node ... out/vs/server/node/server-main.js   (the server)
```

Bash does not forward `SIGTERM` to its children by default, and we don't wait on it before terminating the new generation, so the node child is reparented to PID 1 and continues running. It does eventually exit when its IPC pipe closes, but in our test it persisted for the duration of the run.

## Suggested fix

A couple of options, in rough order of preference:

1. **Signal the whole process group / job** from the CLI side — i.e. set up the server child in its own process group at spawn time (`setsid` / `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP`) and send `SIGTERM` to the negative pgid on shutdown. Robust regardless of what the launch script does.
2. **Make the launcher forward signals**, e.g. `trap` `SIGTERM` / `SIGINT` in `code-server-<quality>` and re-signal `$!` before `wait`. Cheaper change but doesn't help non-shell launchers.
3. **Replace the bash launcher with a direct exec** so the CLI's child *is* the node server. Simplest of all if there's no other reason to keep the shell shim.

## Scope

- CLI only (`cli/src/tunnels/agent_host.rs` and the server-bin shim at `<server>/bin/code-server-<quality>`).
- Independent of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/319455; that PR fixes the desktop-side reconnect after upgrade, and is unaffected by whether the old server's node child gets reaped cleanly.
- Probably also fires on any non-upgrade shutdown path that goes through `kill_running_server` (e.g. supervisor exit / quality switch), so the fix is generally useful.

(Written by Copilot)

input.title: Agent host: "Trying to add a disposable to a DisposableStore that has already been disposed of" from in-process node.js file watcher
input.body:
Seeing this error in the Main process output today, I think it shows when the window starts. Copilot flagged https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/320038 as related cc @DonJayamanne. I think it's harmless but just cleaning it up by fixing the disposable tracking. I'm just wondering whether the watcher churn can be reduced, I don't know anything about the area though.

Copilot text below:

## Problem

The agent host logs the following warning repeatedly:

```
Error: Trying to add a disposable to a DisposableStore that has already been disposed of. The added object will be leaked!
    at r.add (.../agentHostMain.js)
    at r._register (.../agentHostMain.js)
    at r.watch (.../agentHostMain.js)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:104:5)
```

## Root cause

In `NodeJSFileWatcherLibrary.watch()` (`src/vs/platform/files/node/watcher/nodejs/nodejsWatcherLib.ts`):

```ts
this._register(await this.doWatch(stat.isDirectory()));
```

`doWatch()` is async. If the watcher instance is disposed while that promise is in flight, `_store` is already disposed by the time `this._register(...)`  producing the warning and leaking the underlying `fs.watch` handle.runs 

## Why it shows up "a lot" in the agent host

- The agent host runs the non-recursive node.js watcher **in-process** (`NodeJSWatcherClient` instantiates `NodeJSWatcher` directly inside `agentHostMain.js`), so the warning surfaces directly in agent host logs instead of a separate watcher process.
- `SessionCustomizationDiscovery` watches well-known customization roots (workspace root, `.github`, `.claude`, `.copilot`, user home, agents/skills dirs) non-recursively and, on any relevant file change, tears down all those watchers and recreates them on the same paths (debounced). During an active session, files in the workspace root change frequently, so watchers are disposed and recreated  exposing the race above.rapidly 

The watcher churn itself is by design (rescans pick up directories that appear later). The warning + leaked handle is the actual bug.

## Fix

Use the existing `thenRegisterOrDispose` helper so the resolved watch handle is disposed (instead of registered and leaked) when the watcher was disposed during the async `doWatch()`.

## Possible follow-up (optional)

`SessionCustomizationDiscovery.refresh()` disposes *all* watchers on every relevant change and rebuilds them, even for roots that didn't change. Diffing the root set would reduce the churn.

input.title: Copilot session restore splits one interaction into multiple chat responses
input.body:
## Type of Bug

Bug

## Description

Restoring an Agent Host Copilot session can split one SDK interaction into multiple AHP turns and therefore multiple `ChatResponseViewModel` instances. The additional requests have empty prompts.

The Copilot SDK can persist empty `assistant.message` events with no tool requests immediately before another assistant message in the same `interactionId` and SDK `turnId`. The live adapter ignores these non-renderable messages, but `mapSessionEvents` treats every parent assistant message without tool requests as the end of the current turn. The following assistant message then has no active builder, so history replay creates a fallback turn with an empty user message.

The resulting chain is:

1. `mapSessionEvents` prematurely finalizes the parent turn.
2. The next assistant message creates a fallback turn with an empty prompt.
3. `turnsToHistory` emits a request/response pair for every reconstructed turn.
4. `ChatServiceImpl` and `ChatViewModel` create multiple top-level response view models.

Captured restored models showed six empty requests created within the same millisecond. Their request IDs exactly matched the orphaned SDK `assistant.message` event IDs.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run a tool-heavy Copilot Agent Host conversation that produces multiple SDK model rounds.
2. Switch away from the session or restart/reopen it so history is rebuilt from SDK events.
3. Inspect the chat model or rendered transcript.
4. Observe multiple requests/responses with empty prompts for what was one live interaction.

## Expected Behavior

All assistant messages belonging to the same SDK interaction are reconstructed into one AHP turn and one top-level chat response, preserving markdown/tool ordering. Empty, non-renderable assistant events must not create a turn boundary.

## Relevant Code

- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/mapSessionEvents.ts`
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/test/node/mapSessionEvents.test.ts`

(Written by Copilot)

input.title: Agent host: client tool calls in the default chat hang (never resolved)
input.body:
## Problem

Client-provided tool calls running in a session's **default chat** can hang: the SDK handler's deferred is never resolved, so the agent waits forever on a tool result that already came back.

## Root cause

`ChatToolCallComplete` envelopes are emitted on the chat channel URI, which is formatted as:

```
ahp-chat://<chatId>/<base64(sessionUri)>
```

In `agentSideEffects.ts`, both dispatch paths forwarded that chat URI straight into `IAgent.onClientToolCallComplete(session, ...)`. But every agent keys its `_sessions` map by the raw session id, resolved via `AgentSession.id(uri)` = `uri.path.substring(1)`. For a chat URI that path is the **base64 blob**, not the session GUID, so the default-chat lookup never matched and the completion was silently dropped.

This affected the copilot, claude, and codex agents. Peer (additional, non-default) chats happened to still work because they resolve through a separate `_chatSessions` map keyed by the chat URI.

## Fix

Forward the resolved session URI for the default chat (so `AgentSession.id` yields the GUID), and keep forwarding the chat URI for peer chats. Tracked in [#323302](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/323302).

## Follow-ups to consider

- The footgun is that `onClientToolCallComplete(session, ...)` accepts a URI that is sometimes a session URI and sometimes a chat URI, and each agent re-derives routing. Worth considering a clearer contract or a single shared resolution helper so this can't silently regress.
- Add broader coverage that the URI handed to agents at the side-effects boundary is always the one the agent can resolve.

input.title: Agents: Open in Editor should restore Agent Host chat session
input.body:
### Repro steps

1. Start or open an Agent Host session with a worktree in the Agents window.
2. Select **Open in Editor**.

### Actual

VS Code opens the session worktree in a new Editor window, but does not open the corresponding Agent Host chat session.

### Expected

The Editor window should open both the worktree and the corresponding chat session, matching the extension-host Copilot CLI behavior.

### Proposed fix

Pass the session resource through the existing VS Code protocol URL handoff. The main process already forwards its `session` parameter as `vscode:openChatSession` to the target Editor window.

Draft fix: [#323316](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/323316)

(Written by Copilot)

input.title: Selecting a custom agent at session creation is ignored — session starts with the default agent
input.body:
When creating a new session in the Agents window and selecting a custom agent (e.g. **Agents Window Developer**) for a Copilot session from Agent Host, the created session does **not** use the selected custom agent — it starts with the **default** agent instead.

## Steps
1. Open the Agents window → New Sessions view.
2. Select the **vscode** folder using a Copilot session from Agent Host (this is needed to see the **Agents Window Developer** custom agent), and a worktree.
3. Select the **Agents Window Developer** custom agent.
4. Create/start the session.

🐛 The session runs the **default** agent, not the selected custom agent.
